---
title: "00 · Introduction"
subtitle: "CBSE / NCERT Class 11 Mathematics · 14 chapters · Sets, deeper trigonometry, complex numbers, calculus begins"
---

## What this tier is for

This is the **CBSE / NCERT Class 11** track. It walks the rationalised
2023 NCERT textbook chapter by chapter and prepares you for the
Class 12 board year.

Class 11 is the year mathematics broadens dramatically. You meet **sets
and functions** as the language of modern mathematics, you extend
trigonometry from a triangle's angles to the whole real line, you meet
**complex numbers** that solve every quadratic, you count the
permutations of a deck of cards, and you take your first steps into
**calculus**.

By the end of Class 11 you can:

- Use **set notation**, Venn diagrams, and the formulas for
  $|A \cup B|$ and $|A \cup B \cup C|$.
- Define **relations and functions**, classify functions (one-one,
  onto, bijective), and sketch standard graphs.
- Convert between **degree and radian** and use the unit-circle
  definitions of the six trigonometric functions.
- Apply **compound, multiple, and sub-multiple angle identities** and
  solve general **trigonometric equations**.
- Add, multiply, modulus, conjugate, and find polar form of **complex
  numbers**, and solve quadratic equations with complex roots.
- Solve and graph **linear inequalities** in one and two variables.
- Count arrangements with **permutations and combinations** and apply
  the **binomial theorem**.
- Find the $n^{\text{th}}$ term and sum of **arithmetic, geometric**,
  and special series ($\sum n$, $\sum n^2$, $\sum n^3$).
- Compute **slope, equations, distance, and angle between straight
  lines**.
- Identify and graph the four **conic sections** (circle, parabola,
  ellipse, hyperbola).
- Use distance, section formula, and direction cosines in **3D
  geometry**.
- Compute **limits** algebraically and by standard limits, and find
  **derivatives** from first principles and by rules.
- Compute **mean deviation, variance, and standard deviation** of
  grouped data.
- Apply the **axiomatic** definition of probability and compute event
  probabilities using set algebra.

## The lessons

```{mermaid}
flowchart TD
    A[01 · Sets] --> B[02 · Relations & Functions]
    B --> CA[03a · Trig Functions]
    CA --> CB[03b · Trig Identities]
    CB --> CC[03c · Trig Equations]
    D[04 · Complex Numbers]
    E[05 · Linear Inequalities]
    F[06 · Permutations & Combinations] --> G[07 · Binomial Theorem]
    HA[08a · AP & GP] --> HB[08b · Series Sums]
    I[09 · Straight Lines] --> JA[10a · Conics: Circle/Parabola]
    JA --> JB[10b · Conics: Ellipse/Hyperbola]
    K[11 · 3D Geometry Intro]
    LA[12a · Limits] --> LB[12b · Derivatives Intro]
    M[13 · Statistics]
    A --> N[14 · Probability]
```

## Lesson list

1. [**01 · Sets**](01-sets/index.qmd)
2. [**02 · Relations and Functions**](02-relations-and-functions/index.qmd)
3. [**03a · Trigonometric Functions**](03a-trig-functions/index.qmd)
4. [**03b · Compound, Multiple & Sub-Multiple Angle Identities**](03b-trig-identities-compound-angles/index.qmd)
5. [**03c · Trigonometric Equations**](03c-trig-equations/index.qmd)
6. [**04 · Complex Numbers and Quadratic Equations**](04-complex-numbers/index.qmd)
7. [**05 · Linear Inequalities**](05-linear-inequalities/index.qmd)
8. [**06 · Permutations and Combinations**](06-permutations-combinations/index.qmd)
9. [**07 · Binomial Theorem**](07-binomial-theorem/index.qmd)
10. [**08a · Sequences (AP and GP)**](08a-sequences-ap-gp/index.qmd)
11. [**08b · Series and Special Sums**](08b-series-sums/index.qmd)
12. [**09 · Straight Lines**](09-straight-lines/index.qmd)
13. [**10a · Conic Sections (Circle and Parabola)**](10a-conic-sections-circle-parabola/index.qmd)
14. [**10b · Conic Sections (Ellipse and Hyperbola)**](10b-conic-sections-ellipse-hyperbola/index.qmd)
15. [**11 · Introduction to Three-Dimensional Geometry**](11-three-d-geometry-intro/index.qmd)
16. [**12a · Limits**](12a-limits/index.qmd)
17. [**12b · Derivatives (Introduction)**](12b-derivatives-intro/index.qmd)
18. [**13 · Statistics (Variance and Standard Deviation)**](13-statistics/index.qmd)
19. [**14 · Probability (Axiomatic Approach)**](14-probability/index.qmd)

::: {.callout-note}
This page reflects the snapshot of `_curriculum.yml` at the time of build.
Lesson statuses there are the source of truth.
:::
